# Jie-Ting Jiang — Full Portfolio & Resume

> This is the extended version of llms.txt for Jie-Ting Jiang's personal website (https://jtingjiang.com). It contains the full text of all portfolio works, articles, and professional background to provide maximum context for LLM-based analysis.

---

## Professional Summary

Senior Software Engineer specializing in data-intensive applications. I transform complex datasets into intuitive tools, backed by 6+ years of building large-scale BI systems and 3D visualization platforms. From Python / Django-powered APIs to SvelteKit / React / Next.js with Three.js / D3.js frontends, I architect end-to-end solutions that empower 30+ departments to make data-driven decisions. Currently focused on the intersection of AI-assisted engineering and high-performance data visualization.

## Work Experience

### Frontend Engineer
**Pay Web Dev, Line Pay** — September 2025 to Present, Taipei, Taiwan

#### Cross-Framework Migration & AI Tooling
- Led full migration of the Insurance Platform from SvelteKit to Next.js; designed AI-powered code review workflows and spec-driven development processes that cut estimated migration effort by ~70%.
- Introduced AI-assisted development practices (agents, MCPs, prompt workflows) across the engineering team, reducing repetitive tasks and accelerating feature delivery.

#### Line Pay Finance Platform
- Modernize and maintain the Finance Platform, improving long-term stability, performance, and developer experience across the stack.

### Senior Business Intelligence Engineer
**Data Office, Gogoro** — October 2022 to August 2025, Taipei, Taiwan

#### Business Intelligence Platform & Data Visualization
- Built and scaled a BI platform adopted by 30+ departments, making decision science accessible org-wide — from self-serve dashboards to advanced exploratory tools.
- Designed a 3D customer exploration tool (Three.js + Svelte) for querying and visualizing 1M+ records, optimized to run smoothly on average hardware; built a parallel asset management app for 1M+ battery assets.

#### Platform & Developer Experience
- Designed a standardized API framework for data-intensive applications with data engineers, enabling rapid development of exploratory analysis and decision science tools.
- Built shared component libraries (UI, charts, maps) in React and Svelte with designers, accelerating app development and ensuring visual consistency across the platform.

### Data Visualization Engineer
**Network Excellence, Gogoro Network** — March 2019 to September 2022, Taipei, Taiwan

#### Executive Dashboards & Geospatial Analysis
- Built full-stack visualization solutions for executives to monitor 2,500+ GoStations with regional and temporal drill-downs (daily/weekly/monthly).
- Developed an automated geospatial analysis tool with data analysts that cut GoStation site assessment from days to seconds — a 95% reduction in decision turnaround.

#### Architecture Modernization
- Rearchitected a monolithic decision support system into Python-based APIs and microservices, enabling data scientists to ship business logic directly without translation delays — improving debugging, testing, and deployment flexibility.

## Education

- **Master of Science in Informatics** (2015–2017) — Johannes Kepler Universität Linz, Austria. Internship at SKF Group, Steyr. Coursework: Data Visualization, Data Analysis, Machine Learning.
- **Master of Business Administration in Information Management** (2014–2018) — National Sun Yat-Sen University, Taiwan.
- **Bachelor of Science in Computer Science** (2010–2014) — National Sun Yat-Sen University, Taiwan.

## Technical Skills

- **Programming Languages / Frameworks:** Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, SQL, Golang, Django, React, Next.js, Svelte, SvelteKit, Node.js, Express.js
- **Data Visualization & 3D:** D3.js, Three.js, Deck.gl, Google Maps JS API, Mapbox GL JS, openFrameworks
- **DevOps:** GitLab CI, Docker, Kubernetes
- **Specialties:** Large-scale data rendering, geospatial analysis, interactive dashboards, 3D data exploration, AI-assisted development workflows

## Language Skills

English (Full Professional Proficiency), Chinese Mandarin (Native), Taiwanese (Native), German (B1)

## Interests & Hobbies

Infographics, Visual Journalism, Data Humanism, New Media Arts, Specialty Coffee

---

## Selected Works — Full Descriptions

### Claudesay (2026)
**Technologies:** Bash, Claude Code Plugin API
**Links:** [Article](https://jtingjiang.com/works/claudesay) | [Source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/Claudesay)

If you've spent time in a terminal, you've probably run `cowsay` at least once — the old Unix utility that prints a message inside a speech bubble with a cow underneath. It does nothing useful. That's the point.

`claudesay` is cut from the same cloth. It's a plugin for Claude Code that gives the AI assistant a small ASCII cat that reacts to whatever Claude is doing. When Claude edits a file, the cat holds a wrench. When it searches for something, the cat holds a magnifying glass. When Claude finishes a reply and has something to say, it shows up in a speech bubble underneath.

---

### Escaping Flatland (2024)
**Technologies:** Three.js, Svelte, SvelteKit, Octree, GLSL Shaders, LOD
**Links:** [Demo](https://escaping-flatland.netlify.app/) | [Source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/Escaping-Flatland) | [Slides](https://timjjting.github.io/Escaping-Flatland-Slides/)

> *Even though we navigate daily through a perceptual world of three dimensions… the world displayed on our information displays is caught up in the two dimensionality of the endless flatlands of paper and video screens… Escaping this flatland is the essential task of envisioning information — for all the interesting worlds that we seek to understand are inevitably and happily multivariate in nature. Not flatlands. (Tufte 1990)*

Computer screens have the ability to display a wide range of information. Beyond 3D coordinates, they can simultaneously show colors, dynamic patterns, rotations, and motions, enabling human eyes to observe and interpret complex datasets. Zooming further enhances this by allowing both micro and macro readings on the same device, supporting more comprehensive data analysis.

This project demonstrates optimization techniques for plotting large datasets in a 3D space using Three.js and Octree. It also explores how Three.js can be integrated into a Svelte project, though Threlte offers a more mature solution for this purpose.

---

### YouBike Route Explorer (2024)
**Technologies:** Deck.gl, DuckDB, H3, GIS, SvelteKit, Geospatial Analysis
**Links:** [Demo](https://youbike-route-explorer.netlify.app/) | [Source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/YouBike-Route-Explorer)

YouBike, Taiwan's leading public bicycle system, offers 24/7 point-to-point rental and return services, transforming the way residents and visitors navigate urban spaces. With the introduction of YouBike 2.0, featuring a new design for both bikes and stations, the system no longer relies on external electricity, making it more flexible and easier to deploy across the city. This project, YouBike Route Explorer, delves into the deployment strategy of YouBike 2.0 in Taipei City, empowers you to explore usage patterns, popular routes, and how these trends reflect the evolving needs of urban commuters. By "seeing" the data with this exploratory data visualization tool, we can uncover insights that could drive more efficient, accessible, and sustainable transportation solutions.

---

### Task Lineage Generator (2022)
**Technologies:** Golang, D3.js, GraphViz, GitLab CI, Docker, DAG
**Links:** [Demo](https://timjjting.github.io/Task-Lineage-Generator/interface/index.html) | [Source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/Task-Lineage-Generator)

A simple tool for rendering task lineage diagrams written in Golang. The data team has developed a custom-built data pipeline consisting of ETL processes and machine learning models. When a task fails or is delayed, all subsequent tasks must be rerun. The challenge lies in determining the cascading effects of rerunning a task, as the pipeline includes over 250 tasks with complex interdependencies.

The solution represents each task as a node and each dependency as an edge, visualizing the entire pipeline as a graph. The project uses the Golang package goccy/go-graphviz for graph representation and traversal, compiled into a small binary that integrates into GitLab CI pipelines. An interactive web interface built with HTML, CSS, and D3.js provides panning, zooming, highlighting, and tooltips.

Project goals:
- Visualize task lineages as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs)
- Keep the solution as simple as possible
- Seamless integration with existing pipeline
- Automatically refresh the visualization upon task specification (YAML) updates
- Provide interactivity to enhance usability

---

### Chang Yen Tzu (2023)
**Technologies:** Svelte, SvelteKit, CMS, mdsvex, SSR
**Links:** [Visit website](https://changyentzu.com/)

The official website for new media artist Chang Yen Tzu, built entirely with Svelte and SvelteKit, leverages server-side rendering (SSR) for enhanced speed and SEO optimization. This platform is designed with a robust content management system, allowing seamless updates and effortless content organization, making it an ideal showcase for dynamic and interactive artworks.

---

### NBA Teams' Performance Tracking System (2016)
**Technologies:** D3.js, JavaScript, HTML, CSS
**Links:** [Demo](https://timjjting.github.io/NBA-Teams-PTS/) | [Source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/NBA-Teams-PTS)

The NBA Teams' Performance Tracking System is a project that helps users track the performance of NBA teams from 2000 to 2009. It provides interactive D3.js-powered visualizations for multi-dimensional data comparison across seasons.

---

### Sounds of PTT (2018)
**Technologies:** Vue.js, Django, Python, Scrapy, NLP, DSP, Docker, GCP, PostgreSQL, Redis, Celery
**Links:** [Frontend source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/ptt-sounds) | [Backend source](https://github.com/TimJJTing/ptt-sounds-docker-backend)

Sounds of PTT is a web application that can turn an article on PTT (Taiwan's most popular BBS forum) into audible frequencies. The project explores data sonification — What if we could actually "hear" public sentiment orientations? Does it feel different from reading? Can we compose meaningful sounds from public opinions?

How it works: A user enters a PTT article URL. The backend dispatches a crawler which crawls and re-structuralizes article contents. The Sound Maker then processes structuralized data through three steps: tokenization (using jieba for Chinese), quantization (based on sentiment polarity using ANTUSD), and sonification (using ThinkDSP). A media file is generated that represents the sentiment of the article as sound.

The full-stack architecture includes Vue.js frontend, Django REST API backend, Scrapy crawler, PostgreSQL database, Redis caching, Celery task queue, all containerized with Docker and deployed on Google Cloud Compute Engine behind Nginx.

---

### The Paths of Walkers 行者之途 (2022)
**Technologies:** openFrameworks, C++, Microsoft Kinect, openNi2, Motion Detection, Video Rendering
**Links:** [Official page](https://changyentzu.com/works/the-paths-of-walkers-%E8%A1%8C%E8%80%85%E4%B9%8B%E9%80%94)

The Paths of Walkers is an interactive art installation created in collaboration with new media artist Yen Tzu Chang. Exhibited as part of "INCLUDE｜SMA" at Nangang Bottle Cap Factory (Taipei, 2022), audiences can change images with their motions. The project uses openFrameworks for high-quality video rendering and openNi2 with Microsoft Kinect for real-time human motion detection.

The work explores the meaning of freedom through the lens of Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA), examining how patients face their bodies and how freedom exists on both spiritual and physical levels. Visually influenced by medical scenes, the installation reinterprets human skeletons through X-ray imagery while audiences interact through sensors.

- Visual Design: Yen Tzu Chang
- Sound Programming: Yen Tzu Chang
- Interactive Programming: Jie Ting Jiang

---

## Articles & Talks — Full Descriptions

### Data is Beautiful (2024)
**Links:** [Slides](https://timjjting.github.io/Data-is-Beautiful/)

An introductory talk on data visualization principles and practices, originally presented as a guest lecture for college students. Covers why visualization matters and how to approach it thoughtfully.

---

### A Brief Introduction to Common Database Models & Graph Databases (2022)
**Links:** [Slides](https://timjjting.github.io/A-Brief-Introduction-to-Common-Database-Models)

A technical overview of database paradigms including relational, document, and graph models — shared as an internal knowledge-sharing session with engineering colleagues at Gogoro.

---

### React Untersuchungen (2026)

An essay examining React and Next.js through the lens of Ludwig Wittgenstein's philosophy, exploring whether the framework solves problems or creates them.
